buggy_code:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(x, y)
ax2 = ax.twiny()
ax2.plot(x2, y2)
task: Add a second x-axis sharing the same y-axis (twin y, not twin x)
error_location: line 3: twiny() shares x — should use twinx() to share y
clarification_query: Which method creates a second x-axis that shares the same y-axis?
corrected_code:
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.scatter(x, y)
ax2 = ax.twiny()
ax2.plot(x2, y2)
explanation: twiny() creates a new x-axis at the top sharing the y-axis. twinx() creates a new y-axis on the right sharing the x-axis.
